results.title: Dessix: Visual workspace to capture, organize, and create with AI | Product Hunt
results.content:
Visual Alignment ("What you see is what AI sees"): We map your visual focus directly to the AI's context window. If you focus on a specific Block (and its parallel references), that becomes the hard boundary for the AI's attention. You don't need to describe the context; you just need to "place" it in front of you.

Procedural Alignment (Actions & Scenes): This is where the "logic" you mentioned comes in. By encapsulating your intent into Actions (workflows) and Scenes (roles/rules), you are effectively "programming" the AI's behavior. This forces the model to align with your specific methodology rather than relying on its general probability distribution.

In short: We achieve alignment by giving YOU total control over the input structure, rather than hoping the model guesses right. [...] Would love to put Dessix through a proper stress test if you're looking for detailed feedback.

Cruise Chen
Agnes AI 

Agnes AI

Being headache dealing with tons of prompts till now... Dessix is great by putting logic at first place.... also curious - how do you make sure alignments are achieved?

Hsiang (Alex Xiang)
Dessix

Dessix

Hi @cruise\_chen !

Glad you noticed the "logic-first" approach! Dealing with endless prompts is indeed exhausting, and that's exactly why we built Dessix.

To your question on Alignment: We don't treat alignment as magic; we treat it as an engineering problem. We achieve it through Constraint and Transparency: [...] Bowen Tu
HtmlDrag

HtmlDrag

I love the 'visual workspace' approach. Prompting can feel like a black box sometimes, so building context visually makes total sense. Does Dessix allow exporting these visual maps as structured documentation, or is it purely for internal ideation?

Hsiang (Alex Xiang)
Dessix

Dessix

@htmldrag The Column View you see is actually just the tip of the iceberg, mainly for visual alignment.

Behind the scenes, the network self-organizes. We visualize this complete structure in our "Context Map (Overview)" feature—it's a tree view designed specifically to help you verify exactly what the AI knows.

To answer your question: Yes, the final conversation structure and context relationships are fully shareable, exportable, and downloadable.

Rex
Minara

Minara

results.title: AI SEO Score: Check the AI SEO score of your company | Product Hunt
results.content:
Shahriar Hasan
Next3 Offload

Next3 Offload

Thank you for building another great tool for LLM SEO. I am a very big fan and regular user of your tools.   
  
When someone works on optimization, it should be clear what actions to take and why the score is increasing. If this could be shown in a live dashboard where users can optimize and instantly see their updated score, that would be ideal (like Rankmath/Yoast live score). I’d also suggest covering the following components in your tool:

Citation Readiness

Answer Alignment

Knowledge Graph Optimization

Technical Optimization

Competitive Positioning

Peter M. Buch (Buchroithner)
findable.

findable. [...] Peter M. Buch (Buchroithner)
findable.

findable.

@shahriardgm thank you so much for using our LLM SEO tools.   
  
I also appreciate your suggests.  
  
And I agree, there's ton of value in adding some more components to the score. Thanks so much

Bhautik Khunt

@peterbuch @\_\_tosh Which Model Are you using to Analyze ?

Thomas Schranz ⛄️
findable.

findable.

@peterbuch  @bhautikk we use many different models but lately have been using GPT-5 for more content analysis steps in the pipeline where we have used Gemini 2.5 Pro earlier.

Matthew Glossop

Nice! Really straightforward and clear. I did notice that my EEAT score differed significantly from the high-level report to the in-depth view. Is that expected?

Thomas Schranz ⛄️

it just does not work - i registered - put website and it stuck at gap analysis forever. Also i have no idea how gap analysis should work in llm space - as number of questions people ask llms about a product space should be minimum around like 20k questions. Do you run 20k questions to llm to check when it mentioning brand? this will take trillions of tokens.
